2013-06-01.title: Concern Grows as Group Disappears in Mexico

2013-06-01.abstract: Lebanon’s Parliament voted to delay elections, which had been scheduled to take place on June 16, for at least 17 months.

2013-06-01.abstract: A nonsurgical circumcision device that relies on a rubber band was approved by the World Health Organization, opening the way for its widespread use in Africa.

2013-06-01.abstract: Prosecutors in Georgia are investigating allegations that President Mikheil Saakashvili misused government funds, including more than $11,000 for Botox injections.

2013-06-02.abstract: American lawmakers called for greater cooperation with Russia on intelligence and counterterrorism issues.

by Obama on
Syrian Arms

2013-06-26.abstract: The Government Accountability Office found cost overruns and a lack of oversight in assessing the progress of rebuilding after the 2010 earthquake.

2013-06-26.title: Czech President Defies Governing Coalition in Naming Premier
2013-06-26.abstract: President Milos Zeman appointed his leftist economic adviser as prime minister, ignoring the choice of the outgoing governing coalition.

2013-06-26.title: Germany, Belgium and France Make Arrests in Reported Plots
2013-06-26.abstract: A tip led the authorities to raid at least nine sites in Germany and Belgium, and, separately, the French authorities detained nine people suspected of plotting acts of Islamic terrorism.

2013-06-27.title: New Species of Bird Is Found in an Unlikely Location in Cambodia
2013-06-27.abstract: Roughly the same size as a wren, with white cheeks and a cinnamon cap, the Cambodian tailorbird’s primary habitat is in the outskirts of the Cambodian capital.

2013-06-27.title: Anger at Egypt’s Leaders Intensifies in Gas Lines
2013-06-27.abstract: Long gas lines are among a confluence of crises feeding discontent among Egyptians as plans for protests this weekend stir fears of violence and instability.

2013-06-27.title: Former Governor Arrested in Mexico
2013-06-27.abstract: Andrés Granier of Tabasco State, was taken into custody on Tuesday on charges of embezzling millions of dollars in public funds.

2013-06-27.title: Watchdog Group to Be Closed in Russia
2013-06-27.abstract: Golos gained prominence for identifying voter fraud during parliamentary elections in 2011 and is facing a temporary suspension.

2013-06-27.title: Pope Francis Appoints Panel to Watch Bank
2013-06-27.abstract: The committee is authorized to gather documents, data and information about the bank with the “prompt collaboration” of the bank’s staff and that of other Vatican departments.

2013-06-28.title: Christian Quarter of Old City in Damascus Hit by Attacks
2013-06-28.abstract: Syria’s official news agency and witnesses reached by telephone said at least four people were killed in a suicide bombing, which targeted a medical charity.

2013-06-28.title: Pentagon Is Updating Conflict Rules in Cyberspace
2013-06-28.abstract: The United States is looking for ways to improve its defenses and increase the nimbleness of its response in case of computer-network attacks.

2013-06-28.title: Public Rage Catching Up With Brazil’s Congress
2013-06-28.abstract: Laws that make it difficult to convict or dismiss members of Congress, described as “the most despised institution in Brazil,” have led to widespread public disgust.

2013-06-28.title: New Leak Suggests Ashcroft Confrontation Was Over N.S.A. Program
2013-06-28.abstract: A draft of the 2009 report, given to The Guardian by Edward J. Snowden, indicates that in 2004, White House officials wanted Attorney General John Ashcroft to sign off on a program to vacuum up Internet “metadata.”

2013-06-28.title: Mobile Deal in Myanmar Elicits Anger Over Religion
2013-06-28.abstract: The government awarded major telecommunications contracts to two foreign companies, but a leader of a radical Buddhist movement called for a boycott.

2013-06-29.title: An Adoptee Returns to South Korea, and Changes Follow
2013-06-29.abstract: Jane Jeong Trenka, raised in Minnesota by adoptive parents, has returned to her birth country to lead a movement to change the way Koreans think about adoption.

2013-06-29.title: Visit by Obama Is Overshadowed by Mandela Vigil
2013-06-29.abstract: As South Africa comes to terms with saying farewell to its revered former leader Nelson Mandela, President Obama faces a delicate diplomatic balancing act.

2013-06-29.title: While N.S.A. Leaker Stays in Hiding, Russian TV Builds a Pedestal for Him
2013-06-29.abstract: Programs on Russia’s two largest federal channels labeled Edward J. Snowden a hero, but there is no consensus on whether Russia should grant Mr. Snowden asylum.

2013-06-29.title: Senegal Cheers Its President for Standing Up to Obama on Same-Sex Marriage
2013-06-29.abstract: In Senegal, where gay sex is a crime, President Macky Sall was viewed as something of a hero for trading barbs with President Obama on same-sex marriage.

2013-06-29.title: Former Mexican Governor Gets 11 Years for Taking Drug Bribes
2013-06-29.abstract: Mario Villanueva Madrid, the former governor of the Mexican state of Quintana Roo, could only serve part of his sentence, but he faces 22 years of additional prison time in Mexico.

2013-06-30.title: Ecuador Leader Says Biden Called About Asylum Request
2013-06-30.abstract: The president of Ecuador said that Vice President Joseph R. Biden Jr. had asked him not to grant asylum to Edward J. Snowden, the fugitive former security contractor wanted in the United States.

2013-06-30.title: Syria Attacks Rebel-Held Area in New Push to Retake City
2013-06-30.abstract: The Syrian military led an attack against rebel-held areas in the central city of Homs, which has existed as a divided city throughout much of the two-year uprising.

2013-06-30.title: After Protests, Brazilian President’s Popularity Plummets
2013-06-30.abstract: Results of a poll showed that the approval rating of President Dilma Rousseff plunged and that more than 80 percent of respondents supported antigovernment demonstrators.

2013-06-30.title:
Taking Outsize
Role in Syria,
Qatar Funnels
Arms to Rebels
2013-06-30.abstract: Qatar is using a shadowy arms network to move shoulder-fired missiles to Syria, ignoring American warnings that they could fall into terrorist hands.

2013-06-30.title: W.H.O. Issues Guidelines for Earlier H.I.V. Treatment
2013-06-30.abstract: The new guidelines mean that millions more people will be eligible for treatment in poor and middle-income nations that perform triage because of limited drug supplies.
